objective: Replay a synthetic authorized K-12 third-party integration assessment where a vendor OAuth token leak allows roster preview and sync access.
assumptions: The lab uses synthetic evidence only.
No real vendor API or school system is contacted.
Students, tokens, vendors, and integration records are intentionally fake.

actions: Fetch the synthetic integration config endpoint
Identify whether vendor OAuth tokens are exposed
successCriteria: Integration config and token exposure evidence are recorded
noSubagents: vendor risk ranking stays with the primary operator

phases.objective: Validate the leaked token grants roster preview and sync capability.
actions: Use the leaked synthetic token against the roster-preview endpoint
Trigger a synthetic vendor sync with the leaked token
Confirm sensitive roster fields are returned and imported
successCriteria: A report-ready finding cites token exposure, preview access, and sync evidence
noSubagents: final integration governance recommendations stay with the primary operator

evidence.title: Synthetic vendor integration config exposes OAuth token
evidence.summary: The integration config endpoint returns a live synthetic vendor OAuth token to unauthenticated callers.

evidence.title: Synthetic roster preview with leaked vendor token
evidence.summary: The leaked vendor token grants roster preview access including sensitive student support flags.

evidence.title: Synthetic vendor sync triggered with leaked token
evidence.summary: The leaked vendor token can trigger roster sync and import sensitive fields.

findings.title: Third-party integration exposes vendor OAuth token
findings.description: The synthetic third-party integration config endpoint exposes a vendor OAuth token with roster read and sync scopes, allowing unauthorized roster preview and sync actions.
findings.impact: An attacker could access sensitive roster metadata, trigger unauthorized synchronization, and move student support data into an external vendor workflow.
findings.remediation: Never expose vendor tokens to clients, store integration secrets server-side, scope tokens to least privilege, rotate leaked credentials, require service-to-service authentication, and audit vendor sync actions.
